patrick-dan.text: Dan Patrick is a candidate for Lieutenant Governor of Texas, running with Republican.

On taxes, Dan Patrick's recorded position: Dan Patrick has made property tax reduction a central priority, championing increases to the homestead exemption from $15,000 to $100,000 (now $140,000), with higher amounts for seniors. He supports constitutional amendments to make these permanent and has pushed for lowering rollback rates to restrict local government revenue growth.
Dan Patrick is the incumbent in this race. Recorded background: Dan Patrick, incumbent lieutenant governor (2015–present).
As of 2026-02-21, Dan Patrick's campaign had raised $150,000 in campaign receipts and spent $169,407, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total).

goodwin-vikki.text: Vikki Goodwin is a candidate for Lieutenant Governor of Texas, running with Democratic.
On guns, Vikki Goodwin's recorded position: She advocates universal background checks, raising the rifle-purchase age to 21, waiting periods, red-flag laws, and repealing permitless carry.
Vikki Goodwin's recorded background: Vikki Goodwin, state representative from the 47th district (2019–present).
As of 2026-05-16, Vikki Goodwin's campaign had raised $239,668 in campaign receipts and spent $232,357, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total).

collier-mike.text: Mike Collier is a candidate for Lieutenant Governor of Texas, running with Independent.
Mike Collier's recorded background: Mike Collier (Independent), certified public accountant, Democratic nominee for Lt. Governor in 2018 and 2022, Democratic nominee for Comptroller in 2014
As of 2025-12-31, Mike Collier's campaign had raised $50,292 in campaign receipts and spent $42,090, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total).

cristo-anthony.text: Anthony Cristo is a candidate for Lieutenant Governor of Texas, running with Libertarian.
Anthony Cristo's recorded background: Anthony Cristo (Libertarian), nominee for Texas's 15th congressional district in 2018, and nominee for Texas's 34th congressional district in 2020
As of 2025-12-31, Anthony Cristo's campaign had raised $3,750 in campaign receipts and spent $0, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total).

mccormick-kevin.text: Kevin McCormick is a candidate for Lieutenant Governor of Texas, running with Green.
Kevin McCormick's recorded background: Kevin McCormick (Green), former teacher
As of 2025-12-31, Kevin McCormick's campaign had raised $0 in campaign receipts and spent $3,848, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total).

base.summary: Lieutenant Governor of Texas race: Dan Patrick, Vikki Goodwin, Mike Collier, Anthony Cristo, Kevin McCormick. This overview lists each candidate's party, background, and campaign finance where available in our data set.
base.caveats: Our data set for this race covers candidate name, party, incumbency, background summary, and campaign finance totals where a filing was found (federal candidates file with the FEC; Texas state offices file with the Texas Ethics Commission -- each finance bullet names which). We do not have recorded voting histories or issue-by-issue policy positions for these candidates in this data set.

now.text: On taxes, Dan Patrick's recorded position is: "Dan Patrick has made property tax reduction a central priority, championing increases to the homestead exemption from $15,000 to $100,000 (now $140,000), with higher amounts for seniors. He supports constitutional amendments to make these permanent and has pushed for lowering rollback rates to restrict local government revenue growth." That's where Dan Patrick stands right now, this term.

long_term.text: If Dan Patrick's recorded position on taxes holds and becomes sustained policy, it would likely keep shaping this issue the same way over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.
long_term.assumption: Assumes Dan Patrick keeps this position and it turns into lasting policy, rather than changing later or staying just talk.

now.text: On guns, Vikki Goodwin's recorded position is: "She advocates universal background checks, raising the rifle-purchase age to 21, waiting periods, red-flag laws, and repealing permitless carry." That's where Vikki Goodwin stands right now, this term.

long_term.text: If Vikki Goodwin's recorded position on guns holds and becomes sustained policy, it would likely keep shaping this issue the same way over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.

patrick-dan.text: On taxes, Dan Patrick's recorded position: Dan Patrick has made property tax reduction a central priority, championing increases to the homestead exemption from $15,000 to $100,000 (now $140,000), with higher amounts for seniors. He supports constitutional amendments to make these permanent and has pushed for lowering rollback rates to restrict local government revenue growth.

On education, Dan Patrick's recorded position: Dan Patrick has made school choice his signature issue, successfully passing SB 2 in 2025 to establish universal Education Savings Accounts (ESAs) for up to 100,000 Texas students. He has prioritized this issue for over a decade, with the Texas Senate passing school choice bills six times under his leadership. The program prioritizes students with disabilities and lower-income families but is universally available.

As of 2026-02-21, Dan Patrick's campaign had raised $150,000, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total). That is general campaign-finance context; it does not tell us the candidate's stance on property taxes or public school funding.

goodwin-vikki.text: On education, Vikki Goodwin's recorded position: Rep. Goodwin strongly opposes school voucher programs, calling them a 'terrible deal for Texas kids' that diverts billions from public education to unaccountable private schools. She supports raising teacher pay, reducing standardized testing, and keeping public education dollars in public schools.

As of 2026-05-16, Vikki Goodwin's campaign had raised $239,668, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total). That is general campaign-finance context; it does not tell us the candidate's stance on property taxes or public school funding.

He has prioritized this issue for over a decade, with the Texas Senate passing school choice bills six times under his leadership. The program prioritizes students with disabilities and lower-income families but is universally available.

As of 2026-02-21, Dan Patrick's campaign had raised $150,000, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total). That is general campaign-finance context; it does not tell us the candidate's stance on higher education funding or student costs.
